name: Poppy Seed Tante Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the vanilla bean lengthwise and cut off the tips.
2. In a small saucepan, scald milk with vanilla bean.
3. In a small bowl, combine the scalded milk, vanilla bean, and poppy seeds. Let cool to room temperature. Scrape the inside of the vanilla bean into the milk mixture and discard the pod.
4. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.
5. Butter a 9x2 inch round cake pan.
6. In a medium bowl, sift flour, baking powder, and salt.
7. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter on high speed until light and fluffy (2 minutes).
8. Gradually add 1 cup of sugar and continue to beat until very light and creamy (about 5 minutes).
9. Sift in 1/3 of the flour mixture; stir to combine.
10. Beat in half of the milk/poppy seed mixture. Repeat 2 more times with the remaining dry ingredients and milk.
11. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites on medium speed until frothy. Add a pinch of salt and continue beating until soft peaks form (2 minutes). Beat in the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon at a time, increasing the speed to high before adding the last 2 teaspoons. Beat until the meringue is stiff and shiny, about 1 minute.
12. Fold 1/4 of the meringue into the cake batter. Fold in the remaining meringue.
13. Scrape the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 50 to 55 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool for 10 minutes in the pan. Remove from the pan and let cool on a rack.
Use your favorite Cream Cheese frosting. This cake is WONDERFUL and almost pure white. The black poppy seeds add interest to the white cake. From The Best of Food and Wine.
